'''Joseph Shorrocks''' (born 25 November 1999) is a professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|sr}} or {{rlp|lf}} for [[St Helens RFC|St Helens]] in the [[Super League]].

He has spent time on loan from the [[Wigan Warriors]] at the [[London Skolars]] in [[2019 RFL League 1|Betfred League 1]] and at the [[Leigh Leopards]] in [[2023 Super League season|Super League]]. Shorrocks also played for the [[Salford Red Devils]] in the [[Betfred Super League]], and on loan from [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]] at the [[Leeds Rhinos]].

==Background==
Shorrocks was born in [[Billinge Higher End]], [[Metropolitan Borough of Wigan|Wigan, England]]

==Playing career==
===Wigan Warriors===
Shorrocks made his club debut for Wigan in round 10 of the 2019 Super League season against [[Wakefield Trinity]]. Shorrocks made 28 appearances for Wigan in the 2021 Super League season including their playoff loss to [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]]. In the 2022 Super League, Shorrocks played 20 games but did not feature in the clubs 2022 Challenge Cup final victory over [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.wigantoday.net/sport/rugby-league/wigan-warriors-joe-shorrocks-says-hes-excited-to-experience-the-buzz-of-facing-st-helens-at-the-magic-weekend-3758901/|title=Wigan Warriors: Joe Shorrocks says he's excited to experience the buzz of facing St Helens at the Magic Weekend|website=www.wigantoday.net}}</ref>

Shorrocks played 15 games for Wigan in the 2023 Super League season. He did not play in the clubs [[2023 Super League Grand Final]] victory over the [[Catalans Dragons]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2023/oct/14/catalans-dragons-wigan-super-league-grand-final-rugby-league-match-report/|title=Wigan Warriors crowned Super League champions after downing Dragons|website=www.theguardian.com}}</ref>

===Salford Red Devils===
On 20 November 2023, it was reported that Shorrocks had signed for [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]] on a three-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|website=BBC Sport|date=20 Nov 2023|title=Joe Shorrocks: Salford Red Devils sign versatile Wigan Warriors forward on three-year deal|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/67475259}}</ref>

===Leeds Rhinos (loan)===
On 16 August 2025, it was reported that Shorrocks had signed for [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] on loan for the remainder of the 2025 season.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=19 Aug 2025|author=Louis Chapman Coombe|title=Salford Red Devils suffer fresh player exit as cross-Super League switch confirmed|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/salford-red-devils-suffer-fresh-player-exit-as-cross-super-league-switch-confirmed}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|website=All out rugby league|date=16 Aug 2025|title= Leeds Rhinos land Salford Red Devils star as player exodus continues |url =https://www.alloutrugbyleague.co.uk/news/leeds-rhinos-land-salford-red-1332462}}</ref>

===St Helens===
On 14 November 2025 it was reported that he had signed for [[St Helens RFC]] in the [[Super League]] on a one-year deal. Shorrocks made his club debut for St Helens in their 98-2 victory over [[Workington Town]] in round 3 of the 2026 Challenge Cup competition.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=14 Nov 2025|author=Louis Chapman Coombe|title=St Helens tie down Salford Red Devils man as mass exodus continues|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/st-helens-tie-down-salford-red-devils-man-as-mass-exodus-continues}}</ref>
